methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Built Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Noah Ready-Campbell and Andrew Liang, has raised $112M to date. Valuation analysis draws on two applicable methodologies triangulated against a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, comprising one public company and eight private firms including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to a $4M raised proxy, implying $7M. The Peer-Median method references the median valuation of nine robotics and manufacturing peers at $1.6B, with a range from $300M floor to $10.0B ceiling. Notable peers include Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C stage-equivalent), 1X Technologies at $10.0B (Series B), and Exotec at $2.0B (Series D).

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $803M. The substantial spread reflects variance in peer-stage positioning and capital efficiency across the cohort. Key uncertainties include Built Robotics' unit economics relative to higher-valued peers, revenue scale and growth trajectory, and sector-specific risk factors in autonomous systems commercialization. Stage-Progression methodology proves least informative given the early proxy baseline; Peer-Median anchoring dominates the analysis.
